## Overview

**Introduction and Purpose:** This [Document Name] [Policy/Standard] (the "Policy/Standard") outlines [entity name's] approach to [enter description]. The purpose of this [Policy/Standard] is to establish comprehensive guidelines governing Block, Inc., and its U.S. subsidiaries, ensuring consistent operations while maintaining brand-specific flexibility.

**Scope:** This [Policy/Standard] applies to Block, Inc., its subsidiaries, and affiliates (collectively, "Block"), including all products.

## Compliance & Enforcement

### Roles and Responsibilities

**Block, Inc. Roles:**

- **[Role 1]:**
  - Description

- **[Role 2]:**
  - Description

**Subsidiary Roles:**

- **[Role 1]:**
  - Description

### Monitoring and Reporting

On an ongoing basis, the [Policy/Standard] Owner and/or their designee monitors activities for compliance and objective achievement. As outlined in the Compliance Management System Policy, Compliance will provide regular updates, as applicable, to the Block, Inc. Board and executive management. Reporting may cover specific information related to:

- Policies and standards
- Procedures
- Audit/exam findings
- Issues management
- Training
- Complaints

### Training
<!-- OPTIONAL — Include if linked to Mandatory Training Program -->

All relevant personnel will receive training at least annually covering applicable requirements and guidance to implement this [Policy/Standard] effectively. All training related activities are monitored by the Compliance Team and are outlined in the Compliance Training Program.

### Record Retention

All Documents are retained according to the longer of:

- Block's Data Classification & Handling Policy
- Applicable regulatory requirements

### Exceptions

The [Policy/Standard] Owner or designee will have authority to approve exceptions and/or exemptions to this Policy, provided that the proposed exception or exemption does not violate applicable laws and regulations. Exception requests must be documented in writing, include a reason for a request, and provide a description of the controls that the Company will implement to mitigate risks associated with the exception. Any approved exceptions must be documented in writing and retained with recordkeeping requirements. Any approved exceptions or exemptions should be submitted to the Policy Governance Team. The Document Owner and/or designee will inform Compliance Leadership and applicable committees, as needed, of exceptions.

### Confidentiality
<!-- OPTIONAL -->

This document is confidential and should only be accessed by employees.
